questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA)?
options: A) Can be in multiple states simultaneously
B) Can make multiple transitions on a single input
C) Can reject strings but accept strings only after all states have been visited
D) Can accept strings in an infinite number of ways
questions.explanation: A DFA is a finite automaton with a single path between states and can only be in one state at any given time.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a Nondeterministic Finite Automaton (NFA) and a Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA)?
options: A) NFA can only be in one state at a time
B) NFA can make multiple transitions on a single input
C) NFA can accept strings in an infinite number of ways while DFA can only accept strings after all states have been visited
D) NFA can only accept strings
questions.explanation: An NFA can be in multiple states simultaneously and can make multiple transitions on a single input.

questions.question: Which type of finite automaton is used in the application of text search?
options: A) Deterministic Finite Automaton
B) Nondeterministic Finite Automaton
C) Finite Automaton with Epsilon-Transitions
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Text search is typically implemented using a DFA or NFA, but the choice of type depends on the specific requirements of the application.

questions.question: What is the purpose of epsilon-transitions in a Finite Automaton?
options: A) To reduce the language accepted by the automaton
B) To increase the language accepted by the automaton
C) To introduce ambiguity in the language accepted by the automaton
D) To make the automaton more efficient
questions.explanation: Epsilon-transitions allow an automaton to accept an empty string, which can be useful in certain applications.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a string that can be accepted by a Finite Automaton with Epsilon-Transitions?
options: A) 'ab'
questions.explanation: A string like 'a', 'b', '' can be accepted by an automaton with epsilon-transitions because it includes an empty string.
